definition: The science or practice of drawing maps.
example: Advances in satellite technology have greatly improved modern cartography.

definition: A book of maps or charts.
example: She opened the atlas to find the location of the obscure island.

definition: A spherical representation of the earth or of the constellations.
example: He spun the globe and randomly pointed his finger at a country.

definition: An imaginary line drawn around the earth equally distant from both poles, dividing the earth into northern and southern hemispheres.
example: Countries near the equator tend to have tropical climates.

definition: The angular distance of a place north or south of the earth's equator.
example: Toronto is at a latitude of 43 degrees North.

definition: The angular distance of a place east or west of the meridian at Greenwich.
example: The ship's coordinates included its longitude to pinpoint its exact location.

definition: The earth's zero of longitude, which by convention passes through Greenwich, England.
example: The Prime Meridian divides the Earth into Eastern and Western Hemispheres.

definition: A half of the earth, usually as divided into northern and southern halves by the equator, or into western and eastern halves by an imaginary line passing through the poles.
example: Australia is located in the Southern Hemisphere.

definition: Each of a group of numbers used to indicate the position of a point, line, or plane.
example: The pilot entered the coordinates into the navigation system.

definition: An instrument containing a magnetized pointer which shows the direction of magnetic north and bearings from it.
example: The hikers used a compass to find their way out of the dense forest.

definition: The ratio of a distance on the map to the corresponding distance on the ground.
example: According to the map's scale, one inch equals fifty miles.

definition: An explanatory list of the symbols on a map or chart.
example: Check the legend to see what the dotted line represents.

definition: The arrangement of the natural and artificial physical features of an area.
example: The topography of the region includes rolling hills and deep valleys.

definition: An outline, especially one representing or bounding the shape or form of something, often used on maps to denote elevation.
example: Contour lines on a map show the steepness of the terrain.

definition: The representation on a plane surface of any part of the surface of the earth or a celestial sphere.
example: The Mercator projection distorts the size of landmasses near the poles.

definition: The parallel of latitude that is approximately 23.5 degrees north of the equator.
example: The Tropic of Cancer marks the northernmost point where the sun appears directly overhead.

definition: The parallel of latitude that is approximately 23.5 degrees south of the equator.
example: Australia is bisected by the Tropic of Capricorn.

definition: The parallel of latitude that is approximately 66.5 degrees north of the equator.
example: Areas above the Arctic Circle experience the midnight sun in summer.

definition: The parallel of latitude that is approximately 66.5 degrees south of the equator.
example: Few expeditions venture past the icy Antarctic Circle.

definition: A network of lines that cross each other to form a series of squares or rectangles.
example: The city was planned on a neat rectangular grid.

definition: The point in the sky or celestial sphere directly above an observer.
example: At noon, the sun was at its zenith.

definition: The point on the celestial sphere directly below an observer.
example: The nadir is exactly opposite the zenith.

definition: Direction or position relative to a fixed point.
example: The sailor checked the ship's bearing to stay on course.

definition: Height above a given level, especially sea level.
example: The city of Denver is known for its high elevation.

definition: The angular distance of a point north or south of the celestial equator.
example: Astronomers use declination to locate stars in the sky.

definition: The direction of a celestial object from the observer, expressed as the angular distance from the north or south point of the horizon.
example: The telescope was adjusted to the correct azimuth.

definition: The time or date (twice each year) at which the sun crosses the celestial equator, when day and night are of approximately equal length.
example: The vernal equinox marks the beginning of spring.

definition: Either of the two times in the year when the sun reaches its highest or lowest point in the sky at noon, marked by the longest and shortest days.
example: Many ancient cultures celebrated the winter solstice.
